_meta.usage: Maps to the ScriptableObjects in the design doc (ChapterDefinition, GeneratorDefinition, UpgradeDefinition, EventDefinition, Song/CoverDefinition, AlbumPrestige config). Either hand-author SOs from these values or write a small importer. All numbers are starter values for tuning; the Records capstone gate is the primary pacing knob.

currencies.cash: run-scoped; earned by tap + generators; spent on gear/upgrades
currencies.fans: run-scoped; performance meter; sets the album (demo) Records payout; loosely coupled to Cash by design
currencies.rehearsal: run-scoped; fills the Learn Covers bars
currencies.records: PERMANENT; each Record buffs global income; cumulative Records gate the capstone

chapter.name: Garage / Basement
chapter.theme: The band forms in a garage and plays its first backyard show.
chapter.storyBeatOpen: It starts in the garage. Just you, a beat-up amp, and a handful of songs you half-know. Time to make some noise.
chapter.storyBeatCapstone: The backyard's packed. You plug in, count off, and for three minutes the whole neighborhood is yours. Someone's already asking when the next one is — and a guy named Dave offers to haul your gear. You've got your first roadie.

constants.incomeFormulaNote: globalIncome = sum(gen.baseOutput * gen.count * genBuffs) * (1 + 0.02 * records) * encoreBoost. Catalog/Roadie multipliers are not present in Ch1.

sections.name: The Garage Floor
sections.note: No visibleWhen: visible from chapter start. Modules are addressable prefab addresses; sections reveal as a group when their conditions hold (progressive reveal, design doc section 2).

sections.name: The Band
sections.note: Appears with the Stage-0 goal; the practice_amp row unlocks at the same moment. Later sections gate on flags set by content unlocks, e.g. { "type": "flagSet", "flag": "fans" }.

generators.name: Practice Amp
generators.note: First automation. Unlocks the instant the Stage-0 goal (earn 100 Cash by tapping) is met.

generators.name: Drummer
generators.note: First bandmate. Buying the first one triggers the play_for_crowd content unlock (Fans).

upgrades.name: Tight Set
upgrades.note: Example of an upgrade gated on Fans, not Cash — demonstrates the gating web (§4). You must build a crowd, not just money, to buy it.

upgrades.name: Play for a Crowd
upgrades.note: Stage 2. Recruiting your first Drummer means you can perform — unlocks the Fans meter.

upgrades.name: Learn Some Covers
upgrades.note: Stage 3. Unlocks the three Learn Covers bars and the Rehearsal tick.

upgrades.name: Cut a Demo
upgrades.note: Stage 4. Unlocks the Release/Cut-a-Demo (prestige) button. This is the core teaching moment.

rewardsNote: The shared reward pool. Covers and event tiers reference these by id; each type maps to a RewardDefinition subclass asset, so a new reward kind is a new type + entries, and rewards are reusable across content. Available types: fanRateMultiplier, tapValueMultiplier, setFlag (flag: <id> — lets a reward reveal gated content).

rehearsal.note: Rehearsal points fill cover bars. Passive tick plus taps, so covers advance from engagement, not from Cash.

covers.note: Run-scoped: reset each demo but refill faster as the run gets stronger. Cover_1 is on the critical path (gates cut_demo). Cover buffs stack multiplicatively on fan rate.

fans.perBandmateNote: Applies per owned unit of bandmate generators (drummer/bassist/guitarist), not practice_amp. Ties fan rate to band size.
fans.couplingNote: fanRate is intentionally NOT a function of Cash or Cash/sec — only band size, covers, and time — so income alone cannot shortcut the demo payout (§11).

album.note: Releasing resets the run and awards Records. Each Record = +2% permanent global income, so the next run is faster.

events.name: Garage Jam Challenge
events.availableNote: Introduced in Stage 6, after the player has released at least one demo, so the prestige loop is understood first.
events.baselineResetNote: On entry the chapter economy resets to a fixed baseline for the event's duration, so tiers scale independently of the player's Records.
events.failNote: On a timed fail the event resets; the player can quit anytime. Cost is time only, never permanent progress.
events.tuningNote: Reward magnitude is the dial that sets how essential this event is (§6.1). As written it's a helpful-but-skippable tap booster; raise the reward to make it soft-required for reasonable pacing.

onComplete.roadieNote: First Roadie is a story reward here. The allocation/replay system stays locked until Chapter 2.

balanceTargets.primaryPacingKnob: capstoneRecordsGate — raise/lower before touching generator or fan curves
balanceTargets.tuneAgainst: a player with full multiplier stack active (here just Encore up to 4x + records); confirm the chapter still takes meaningful play time
